setting.note: The physician Sinuhe tells the story of his life across the turbulent reign of the heretic pharaoh Akhenaten in fourteenth-century-BC Egypt. Waltari anchors it in the real ancient geography of the Nile: Thebes with its great temple of Amun, the House of Death across the river where Sinuhe learns his craft, Akhenaten's new sun-city of Akhetaten raised in the desert, the old capital of Memphis, and the far cities of the east to which Sinuhe's wanderings carry him.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Mika Waltari, first published 1945 (Sinuhe egyptiläinen). Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Egyptian, Mika Waltari (1945). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author's estate or publisher.

palette.note: The gold of the pharaohs and of the sun-disc Aten whose worship convulses the kingdom. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
